name: Thai Turkey Bites
recipeIngredient: 1 medium cucumber, cut half-lengthwise and into 1/4-inch thick slices
1 tbsp red curry paste (or 1 tsp chili powder and 1/2 tsp sesame oil)
recipeInstructions:
For the sauce: In a mixing bowl, combine boiling water, brown sugar, and lime juice. Stir until the brown sugar dissolves. Add cucumber slices, onion, and chili pepper. Mix well. Cover and chill for up to 3 days.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground turkey, fish or soy sauce, and red curry paste (or chili powder and sesame oil). Mix well. Shape turkey mixture into 1-inch meatballs and flatten slightly. Arrange meatballs in a microwave-safe 8x8x2 inch baking dish. Cover the dish loosely with waxed paper.
Microwave on 100% power for 5 to 7 minutes, or until done, turning meatballs over and rotating the dish halfway through cooking. To serve, place meatballs in a chafing dish over burner. Transfer sauce to serving bowl. With a toothpick, spear a cucumber slice, then a meatball. Dip into sauce. Makes 25 to 30 meatballs. Meatballs can be frozen.
